name: unicode-nfc
description: Unicode NFC normalization requirements per pack.md Section 2.3.1.1

tests.name: nfc_form_valid
tests.description: NFC form (e-acute as single codepoint U+00E9) is valid

tests.name: nfd_form_invalid
tests.description: NFD form (e + combining acute U+0065 U+0301) MUST be rejected because NFC(path) != path

tests.name: hangul_nfc_valid
tests.description: Hangul syllable GA (precomposed NFC form) is valid

tests.name: hangul_nfd_invalid
tests.description: Hangul Jamo sequence (decomposed NFD) MUST be rejected

tests.name: angstrom_nfc_valid
tests.description: Latin capital A with ring above (NFC form) is valid

tests.name: angstrom_nfd_invalid
tests.description: A + combining ring above (NFD) MUST be rejected

tests.name: ohm_nfc_valid
tests.description: Greek capital omega (NFC canonical form) is valid

tests.name: ohm_symbol_invalid
tests.description: Ohm sign U+2126 normalizes to U+03A9, so MUST be rejected

tests.name: fi_ligature_valid
tests.description: fi ligature U+FB01 does not change under NFC, so is valid

tests.name: multiple_combining_marks_invalid
tests.description: o + circumflex + dot below reorders under NFC normalization

tests.name: fullwidth_valid
tests.description: Fullwidth letters do not change under NFC (only NFKC)

implementation_notes.rationale: Prevents homograph attacks and ensures consistent path matching across platforms with different Unicode normalization defaults (e.g., macOS HFS+ uses NFD)

notes: Per pack.md Section 2.3.1.1: Paths MUST be in Unicode NFC form. Implementations MUST reject paths that change under NFC normalization.
